 {"id":"bd-5z3e","title":"E2E Tests: Agent-to-agent handoff workflow","description":"E2E Tests for Agent-to-agent handoff workflow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic handoff
   - Spawn agent A, work on task partially
   - Kill agent A, spawn agent B with handoff
   - Verify context received
   - Verify B continues work

2. ntm handoff command
   - Run ntm handoff myproject cc_1 cc_2
   - Verify state captured from cc_1
   - Verify state injected to cc_2
   - Verify cc_2 acknowledges

3. Automatic handoff on crash
   - Enable auto-handoff
   - Simulate agent crash
   - Verify replacement spawned
   - Verify context transferred

4. Multi-hop handoff
   - Agent A -> Agent B -> Agent C
   - Verify context accumulates
   - Verify no info loss

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-HANDOFF] Capturing state from SOURCE: N bytes
[E2E-HANDOFF] State includes: N decisions, N files, N blockers
[E2E-HANDOFF] Injecting to TARGET
[E2E-HANDOFF] Target acknowledged handoff
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic handoff preserves work context
3. ntm handoff command captures and injects state
4. Auto-handoff triggers on agent crash
5. Multi-hop preserves accumulated context
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-HANDOFF] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify agent-to-agent handoff
2. Handoff message delivered correctly
3. Context transferred between agents
4. Handoff logged in audit trail
5. Test cleanup removes test sessions
6. Test duration under 5 minutes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:16:47.931743771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:20:19.541514903Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5z3e","depends_on_id":"bd-2i44","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-5z3e","depends_on_id":"bd-2ofm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-64v8u","title":"RCH: Add dashboard widget for build offload status","description":"Add dashboard widget showing RCH build offload status.

## Widget Design

### Compact View
```
┌─ RCH Build Offload ─────────────┐
│ 🟢 45 builds (30 remote)        │
│ Time saved: ~20 min             │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Expanded View
```
┌─ RCH Status ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Workers:  builder-1 (🟢)  builder-2 (busy)       │
│                                                   │
│ Session Stats:                                    │
│   Total builds:     45                            │
│   Remote:           30 (67%)                      │
│   Local (fallback): 15 (33%)                      │
│   Time saved:       ~20 minutes                   │
│                                                   │
│ Current: cargo build --release on builder-2      │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard widget shows build offload status
2. Displays local vs remote build counts
3. Shows time saved via offloading
4. Worker health status visible
5. Real-time updates during builds
6. Unit tests for widget rendering
7. E2E test verifying accurate display","acceptance_criteria":"1. Dashboard widget shows build offload status
2. Displays local vs remote build counts
3. Shows time saved via offloading
4. Worker health status visible
5. Real-time updates during builds
6. Unit tests for widget rendering
7. E2E test verifying accurate display","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:08:00.775021376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:10:50.341046186Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-64v8u","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-64v8u","depends_on_id":"bd-1ghcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-64v8u","depends_on_id":"bd-3u84u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-69cuc","title":"Task: Add TOON tool adapter (detect/version)","description":"## Overview
If a TOON CLI is used, add a tools adapter for detecting the `tru` binary and version.

## Details
- CLI is provided by toon_rust (binary: `tru`).
- Prefer `tru --version` for version parsing; fallback to `tru --help` if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects installed `tru` binary
- Parses version output
- Used by `--robot-format=auto` to decide availability","notes":"UPDATE per bd-2csfi decision: This task is no longer needed.

Decision was made to use Go library (toon-format/toon-go) instead of CLI wrapper. Since there's no CLI binary to detect, this tool adapter task is unnecessary.

Recommend closing as 'not-needed' or 'superseded'.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:35.705545571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T21:01:28.613256364Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T01:47:06.679986348Z","close_reason":"Obsolete: TOON encoding is in-process Go; no external toon CLI to detect.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-69cuc","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-69cuc","depends_on_id":"bd-2csfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"bd-69cuc","author":"QuietCanyon","text":"Clarification: Node @toon-format/cli is not allowed; this task remains obsolete due to tru-only/in-process approach.","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:57:56Z"},{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-69cuc","author":"QuietCanyon","text":"Updated description to tru-only (no Node CLI mention).","created_at":"2026-01-24T21:01:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-6fek","title":"Error Handling Tests: ntm init recovery scenarios","description":"Error Handling Tests for ntm init recovery scenarios.

## Error Scenarios to Test

1. Filesystem errors
   - Permission denied on directory creation
   - Disk full during config write
   - Read-only filesystem
   - Network filesystem latency

2. Config validation errors
   - Invalid TOML syntax recovery
   - Missing required fields
   - Type mismatches in config
   - Circular template references

3. Graceful degradation
   - Continue with defaults if config unreadable
   - Warn but proceed if optional dir creation fails
   - Fallback if flywheel tool unavailable

## Recovery Verification
- Test that partial init can be resumed
- Test that corrupted config is detected and reported
- Test cleanup on failure (no orphaned files)

## Logging Requirements
```
[ERROR] OPERATION: ERROR_MESSAGE
[RECOVER] Attempting: RECOVERY_ACTION
[CLEANUP] Removing: PATH
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 3 error scenario categories tested
2. Filesystem errors handled gracefully
3. Config validation errors reported clearly
4. Graceful degradation falls back to defaults
5. Partial init can be resumed
6. Failed init cleans up orphaned files
7. Detailed logging with [ERROR]/[RECOVER]/[CLEANUP] prefixes","acceptance_criteria":"1. Error handling tests for ntm init
2. Recovery from partial init tested
3. Config file corruption handled
4. Permission errors handled
5. Test cleanup removes test configs
6. Test duration under 3 minutes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:22.883192270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:38.036344598Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-ubhl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6mls6","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble presets' command for preset management","description":"Implement `ntm ensemble presets` command to list available ensembles (built-in + user-defined).

## Command Specification
```
ntm ensemble presets [flags]
  --format string   Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose         Show full ensemble configurations
```

- `ntm ensemble presets` is an alias for `ntm ensemble list` (shared implementation).
- Primary UX language: **ensemble**; “presets” is a convenience alias.

## Output Requirements
- Include `name`, `display`, `description`, `modes` (id + code), synthesis strategy, budget, tags
- Include `allow_advanced` flag when present
- In verbose mode, expand modes to include names, categories, and tiers

## Dependencies
- Built-in ensembles (bd-cph20)
- Ensemble loader (bd-1ovi8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lists built-in + user-defined ensembles
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs are deterministic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:28.550161191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:36:42.595437577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:36:42.595344604Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm ensemble presets command with table/json/yaml formats, verbose mode, and tag filtering. Added unit tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6mls6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ovi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6mls6","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6mls6","depends_on_id":"bd-cph20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6qk8","title":"Task: Implement ntm level command","description":"## Overview
Create the 'ntm level' command for viewing and changing proficiency tier.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/level.go:
   - ntm level - show current tier and progress
   - ntm level up - promote to next tier
   - ntm level down - demote to previous tier
   - ntm level set <tier> - set specific tier (1, 2, 3 or apprentice, journeyman, master)

2. Output format for 'ntm level':
   Current proficiency: Journeyman (Tier 2)
   
   Usage stats:
     Commands run: 150
     Sessions created: 25
     Prompts sent: 200
     Using NTM since: 15 days
   
   Next tier: Master
     Unlocks: Robot mode, internal debugging, experimental features
     Suggested milestone: Run 50 more commands or 30 days of use
   
   Run 'ntm level master' to unlock all features now.

3. Promotion flow:
   - Confirm before tier change
   - Show what changes (new commands unlocked or hidden)
   - Log promotion to history

## Implementation Notes
- Use cobra subcommands for level operations
- Support both numeric (1,2,3) and named (apprentice, journeyman, master) tiers
- Add emoji support when terminal supports it

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm level shows current status
- ntm level up/down work correctly
- Promotion history is recorded
- Unit tests for all subcommands","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:53.536644317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:11:16.075203473Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:11:16.075154591Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm level command in internal/cli/level.go with show/up/down/set subcommands, added to root.go and tiers registry","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qk8","depends_on_id":"bd-1s9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6qk8","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6s038","title":"TOON: Update documentation and examples","description":"Update README and other documentation for TOON support.

## Documentation Updates

### README.md
- Add TOON output format to robot mode section
- Include examples with both JSON and TOON output
- Document performance benefits

### Example Updates
```bash
# JSON output (default)
ntm --robot-list-panes
{"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}

# TOON output (40-60% fewer tokens)
ntm --robot-list-panes --robot-output-format toon
panes:[{name:agent-1,program:claude-code,active:T}]
```

### Configuration Documentation
Document [robot.output] section with all options.

### Migration Guide
For users who have scripts parsing JSON output:
- JSON remains default
- Explicit --robot-output-format json for guaranteed JSON
- Recommendation: update scripts to support TOON for cost savings

## Code Location
- README.md
- docs/ (if exists)
- examples/ (if exists)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. README.md updated with TOON output format section
2. Examples show both JSON and TOON output side-by-side
3. Configuration documentation includes [robot.output] section
4. Migration guide explains backwards compatibility
5. Performance benefits documented with token savings estimates","acceptance_criteria":"1. README documents TOON format
2. Examples show before/after JSON vs TOON
3. Config documentation updated
4. Migration guide for existing users
5. All robot mode docs mention TOON option","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:04:31.896361813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:13:07.422621286Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6s038","depends_on_id":"bd-1s6cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6s038","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
